methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $137M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Arc Boats is a Series B transportation company founded by Mitch Lee that has raised $137M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies—Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M)—given the absence of public comparables in this space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $260M. Peer-median methodology anchors to the $500M median valuation across the 3-company sample, with a range spanning $270M to $1.8B. The stage-progression and peer-median methods are both applicable given Arc's positioning within the transportation frontier-tech universe.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a valuation range of $221M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $380M. The $260M stage-progression result sits below the peer median, reflecting Arc's earlier stage relative to two Series C comparables; the $500M peer median reflects the broader market value observed in the transportation sector. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of a 3-company peer set spanning two stages, and the appropriateness of using total capital raised as a valuation proxy absent post-investment valuations from recent financing rounds.
